LAKEWOOD YACHT CLUB SEAHORSE NEWSLETTER
Volume 41, Issue 9 | September 2024

FROM THE HELM
Commodore Patricia Harmon

Dear members and sailing families,

Fall is upon us and what a season it has been. Our junior sailors have competed
in seven regattas since May, bringing home trophies from the Texas Youth Race
Week, the Galveston Bay Junior Series, and the Gulf Coast Opti Invitational.

I am delighted to share that this season, LYC expanded our scholarship program
to cover six junior sailor families — double the number from spring. We remain
committed to making sailing accessible to all Seabrook area families.

FROM THE DESK OF THE JUNIOR SAILING DIRECTOR
Youth Program Fall 2024 Update

This fall's junior sailing program has seen remarkable growth. We now have 58
sailors enrolled across all divisions — a 10% increase from last year. The C420
fleet, introduced in spring 2024, now has 16 sailors and two dedicated coaches.

Coach Maria Santos and Coach David Nguyen continue to lead our Opti fleet with
excellent results. The ILCA division welcomed new assistant coach Rosa Chen this
fall, who brings national-level racing experience to our program.

Registration for spring 2025 will open November 1, 2024. Early-bird pricing
will be in effect through November 15. The junior sailing committee is exploring
expansion of the scholarship fund for 2025 — more details at the October
general meeting.

TEXAS YOUTH RACE WEEK 2024 RECAP

The LYC Seahorse racing team sent 14 sailors to Texas Youth Race Week (TYRW),
held July 7-12 at Houston Yacht Club. Results:

Optimist Championship Fleet:
- Marco Torres: 3rd overall (best LYC Opti result in 6 years)
- Lucy Whitfield: 7th overall

C420 Division (first year competing as a team):
- Abby Kim / Raj Patel: 5th overall

ILCA Division:
- Connor O'Brien: 8th overall

The team's combined performance earned LYC the "Most Improved Club" award at
the TYRW closing ceremony. Head coach Maria Santos was recognized for her work
with the Opti fleet.

RACING RESULTS — SEPTEMBER
Wednesday Night Series, Races 4-6

PHRF A Fleet:
1. Persistence (J/105) — Bill Murray
2. Blue Smoke (Melges 24) — Sandra Chen
3. Windfall (Hunter 40) — Tom Davis

Optimist Fleet — September Intramural:
1. Marco Torres
2. Lucy Whitfield
3. Finn Harmon

C420 — September Club Series:
1. Abby Kim / Raj Patel
2. Connor O'Brien / Lily Nguyen

UPCOMING EVENTS
Fall Calendar

September 28: Fall Work Party — racing mark maintenance
October 5: Fall Regatta — Galveston Bay Open
October 12: Junior Sailing Awards Dinner ($25/person, families welcome)
October 19: Halloween Costume Sail — costume judging at 4pm, prizes for best
October 26: Clam Bake and Jam — annual cookout, live music
November 2: Last day of junior sailing season
November 9: Annual Meeting and Board Elections

MEMBER NEWS

Congratulations to the Whitfield family — Lucy Whitfield has been selected to
the US Sailing Youth Team development program. LYC is enormously proud.

Welcome to our newest members: the Brennan family (Seabrook), the Ortega
family (Clear Lake Shores), and the Johansson family (Webster).

COMMITTEE REPORTS
Fleet Captain Report — Keel Boat Fleet

The keel boat fleet has had an excellent racing season. We welcome two new
boats to the club fleet: a J/88 (donated in memory of George Patterson by his
family) and a Melges 24 available for junior and young adult racing.

Fair winds,
Patricia Harmon, Commodore
